what: Use as_forest_data() to standardize a coefficient table
title: Prepare Forest Data with Helper Functions
text: as_forest_data() converts column names internal structure used ggforestplotR. result contains columns expected ggforestplot(), add_forest_table(), add_split_table(). data standardized, can pass straight ggforestplot().
code: raw_coefs <- data.frame(   variable = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   beta = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   lower = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   upper = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56),   display = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Treatment"),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98),   p_value = c(0.04, 0.15, 0.001) )  forest_ready <- as_forest_data(   data = raw_coefs,   term = "variable",   estimate = "beta",   conf.low = "lower",   conf.high = "upper",   label = "display",   grouping = "section",   n = "sample_size",   p.value = "p_value" ) ggforestplot(forest_ready) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Forest plot from standardized coefficient data")

title: Prepare Forest Data with Helper Functions
text: broom available, tidy_forest_model() can pull coefficient estimates confidence limits fitted model. returned object can passed directly ggforestplot().  logistic regression, set exponentiate = TRUE return odds ratios instead log-odds coefficients.
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars)  model_ready <- tidy_forest_model(fit) ggforestplot(model_ready) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Forest plot from tidy_forest_model() output") set.seed(123)  logit_data <- data.frame(   age = rnorm(250, mean = 62, sd = 8),   bmi = rnorm(250, mean = 28, sd = 4),   treatment = factor(rbinom(250, 1, 0.45), labels = c("Control", "Treatment")) )  linpred <- -9 +   0.09 * logit_data$age +   0.11 * logit_data$bmi +   0.9 * (logit_data$treatment == "Treatment")  logit_data$event <- rbinom(250, 1, plogis(linpred))  logit_fit <- glm(event ~ age + bmi + treatment, data = logit_data, family = binomial()) logit_ready <- tidy_forest_model(logit_fit, exponentiate = TRUE) ggforestplot(logit_ready) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Forest plot from exponentiated logistic regression output",     x = "Odds ratio"   )

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: Use package one three ways: Start coefficient table map required columns directly. Start fitted model let tidy_forest_model() ggforestplot() call broom::tidy(). Add add_forest_table() add_split_table() styling main plot. article covers basic entry points minimum data need.

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: simplest input data frame term, estimate, confidence limits.  columns use different names, map explicitly.
code: basic_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  ggforestplot(basic_coefs) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Basic forest plot") renamed_coefs <- data.frame(   variable = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   beta = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   lower = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   upper = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  ggforestplot(   renamed_coefs,   term = "variable",   estimate = "beta",   conf.low = "lower",   conf.high = "upper" ) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Forest plot with remapped columns")

what: Add grouped sections and row striping
title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: Use grouping want related variables separated labeled panels. Add striped_rows = TRUE larger display benefits clearer row tracking.
code: sectioned_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III", "Nodes"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.20, 0.34, 0.52, 0.28),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.05, 0.12, 0.20, 0.06),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.35, 0.56, 0.84, 0.50),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor", "Tumor") )  ggforestplot(   sectioned_coefs,   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "grey94" ) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Grouped forest plot with striped rows")

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: Use add_forest_table() finish main ggplot2 styling.
code: tabled_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III"),   estimate = c(0.12, -0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 0.46),   conf.low = c(0.03, -0.18, 0.04, 0.10, 0.18),   conf.high = c(0.21, -0.02, 0.32, 0.50, 0.74),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98, 87, 83) )  ggforestplot(tabled_coefs, n = "sample_size", striped_rows = TRUE) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Forest plot with a left-side summary table") +   add_forest_table(     position = "left",     show_n = TRUE,     estimate_label = "Beta"   )

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: Use add_split_table() term columns stay left summary statistics move right.
code: ggforestplot(tabled_coefs, n = "sample_size") +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Forest plot with split tables") +   add_split_table(     left_columns = c("term", "n"),     right_columns = c("estimate")   )

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: broom installed, ggforestplot() can work directly fitted model.
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars)  ggforestplot(fit, sort_terms = "descending") +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Forest plot directly from an lm() object")

title: Get Started with ggforestplotR
text: detail, see: ggforestplotR-plot-customization styling, separators, table layouts. ggforestplotR-data-helpers as_forest_data() tidy_forest_model().

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III"),   estimate = c(0.12, -0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 0.46),   conf.low = c(0.03, -0.18, 0.04, 0.10, 0.18),   conf.high = c(0.21, -0.02, 0.32, 0.50, 0.74),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98, 87, 83),   p_value = c(0.04, 0.15, 0.29, 0.001, 0.75),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor") )

what: Group rows and control strip placement
title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: grouping creates section panels, grouping_strip_position controls side gets strip labels.
code: ggforestplot(   coefs,   grouping = "section",   grouping_strip_position = "right",   striped_rows = TRUE ) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Grouped forest plot with right-side strips")

what: Add separator lines around a multi-level variable
title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: Use separator_group separator_lines categorical variable expands several levels want visible boundaries around block.
code: race_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("race_black", "race_white", "race_other", "age", "bmi"),   label = c("Black", "White", "Other", "Age", "BMI"),   estimate = c(0.24, 0.08, -0.04, 0.12, -0.09),   conf.low = c(0.10, -0.04, -0.18, 0.03, -0.17),   conf.high = c(0.38, 0.20, 0.10, 0.21, -0.01),   variable_block = c("Race", "Race", "Race", "Age", "BMI") )  ggforestplot(   race_coefs,   label = "label",   separator_group = "variable_block",   separator_lines = TRUE,   striped_rows = TRUE ) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Separator lines around a multi-level Race variable")

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: add_forest_table() simpler composition helper summary columns sit one side plot. Add last, main plot styling, returns patchwork composition rather plain ggplot.
code: ggforestplot(   coefs,   grouping = "section",   n = "sample_size",   p.value = "p_value",   striped_rows = TRUE ) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Left-side summary table") +   add_forest_table(     position = "left",     show_n = TRUE,     show_p = TRUE,     estimate_label = "Beta"   )

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: add_split_table() better term labels stay left formatted statistics move right. Like add_forest_table(), added plot-level styling.  can also specify split-table columns position instead name.
code: ggforestplot(   coefs,   n = "sample_size",   p.value = "p_value",   striped_rows = TRUE ) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Split table layout") +   add_split_table(     left_columns = c("term", "n"),     right_columns = c("estimate", "p"),     estimate_label = "Beta"   ) ggforestplot(   coefs,   n = "sample_size",   p.value = "p_value" ) +   add_split_table(     left_columns = c(1, 2),     right_columns = c(3, 4),     estimate_label = "Beta"   )

what: Plot odds ratios from logistic regression
title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: logistic regression models, use exponentiate = TRUE x-axis odds-ratio scale null reference stays 1.  can attach table well relabel estimate column odds ratios explicitly.
code: set.seed(123)  logit_data <- data.frame(   age = rnorm(250, mean = 62, sd = 8),   bmi = rnorm(250, mean = 28, sd = 4),   treatment = factor(rbinom(250, 1, 0.45), labels = c("Control", "Treatment")) )  linpred <- -9 +   0.09 * logit_data$age +   0.11 * logit_data$bmi +   0.9 * (logit_data$treatment == "Treatment")  logit_data$event <- rbinom(250, 1, plogis(linpred))  logit_fit <- glm(event ~ age + bmi + treatment, data = logit_data, family = binomial()) ggforestplot(logit_fit, exponentiate = TRUE) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Odds ratios from a logistic regression model",     x = "Odds ratio"   ) ggforestplot(logit_fit, exponentiate = TRUE) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Logistic regression with an odds-ratio table") +   add_forest_table(position = "right", estimate_label = "OR", show_p = TRUE)

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: group argument multiple estimates row.
code: comparison_coefs <- data.frame(   term = rep(c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III"), 2),   estimate = c(0.12, -0.10, 0.18, 0.30, 0.46, 0.08, -0.05, 0.24, 0.40, 0.58),   conf.low = c(0.03, -0.18, 0.04, 0.10, 0.18, 0.00, -0.13, 0.10, 0.20, 0.30),   conf.high = c(0.21, -0.02, 0.32, 0.50, 0.74, 0.16, 0.03, 0.38, 0.60, 0.86),   model = rep(c("Model A", "Model B"), each = 5),   section = rep(c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor"), 2) )  ggforestplot(   comparison_coefs,   group = "model",   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   dodge_width = 0.5 ) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Comparing two model specifications")

title: Customize Forest Plots and Tables
text: returned object still normal ggplot add table helper.
code: ggforestplot(   comparison_coefs,   group = "model",   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "#F3F4F6",   zero_line_colour = "#9CA3AF",   zero_line_linetype = 3,   point_size = 2.8,   line_size = 0.6 ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Customized grouped forest plot",     x = "Effect estimate",     subtitle = "Clinical and tumor terms shown in separate sections"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Model A" = "#1D4ED8", "Model B" = "#D97706")   ) +   ggplot2::theme(     legend.position = "top",     panel.grid.major.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),     strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "#E5E7EB", colour = NA),     strip.text.y.left = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),     plot.title.position = "plot"   )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Carson Richardson. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Richardson C (2026). ggforestplotR: Build Forest Plots Model Coefficients ggplot2. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggforestplotR: Build Forest Plots from Model Coefficients with ggplot2},   author = {Carson Richardson},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/}, }

title: ggforestplotR
text: ggforestplotR provides ggplot2-first workflow building forest plots tidy coefficient tables fitted model objects.

title: ggforestplotR
text: Install current development version GitHub.
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("thatoneguy006/ggforestplotR")

title: ggforestplotR
text: package currently supports three common workflows: Build forest plot directly coefficient table Start fitted model tidy plotting broom Add side tables split-table layouts reporting-ready output

title: ggforestplotR
code: library(ggforestplotR) library(ggplot2)  sectioned_coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Smoking", "Stage II", "Stage III", "Nodes"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.20, 0.34, 0.52, 0.28),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.05, 0.12, 0.20, 0.06),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.35, 0.56, 0.84, 0.50),   section = c("Clinical", "Clinical", "Clinical", "Tumor", "Tumor", "Tumor") )  ggforestplot(   sectioned_coefs,   grouping = "section",   striped_rows = TRUE,   stripe_fill = "grey94" ) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Grouped forest plot with striped rows")

title: ggforestplotR
text: Get started: https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/articles/ggforestplotR-get-started.html Plot & Table customization: https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/articles/ggforestplotR-plot-customization.html Data helpers: https://thatoneguy006.github.io/ggforestplotR/articles/ggforestplotR-data-helpers.html

title: ggforestplotR
text: ggforestplot() builds plotting panel data frame supported model object. add_forest_table() attaches summary table left right side plot. add_split_table() creates traditional forestplot tables sides plot. as_forest_data() standardizes custom coefficient data. tidy_forest_model() converts fitted models plotting-ready coefficient data.

what: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
text: Compose side table onto forest plot plot styled. helper generally added final step returns patchwork composition instead plain ggplot.

title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
code: add_forest_table(   plot = NULL,   position = c("left", "right"),   show_terms = TRUE,   show_n = NULL,   show_estimate = TRUE,   show_p = FALSE,   term_header = "Term",   n_header = "N",   estimate_label = "Estimate",   p_header = "P-value",   digits = NULL,   text_size = NULL,   striped_rows = NULL,   stripe_fill = NULL,   stripe_colour = NULL,   grid_lines = FALSE,   grid_line_colour = "black",   grid_line_size = 0.3,   grid_line_linetype = 1 )

title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
text: plot plot created ggforestplot(). Leave NULL use + add_forest_table(...) syntax. position Whether place table left right forest plot. show_terms Whether show term column table. show_n Whether show N column. Defaults TRUE underlying plot data include n column. show_estimate Whether show formatted estimate confidence interval column. show_p Whether show p-value column right estimate column. term_header Header text term column. n_header Header text N column. estimate_label Header label estimate column. p_header Header text p-value column. digits Number digits used formatting estimates p-values. Defaults 2. text_size Text size table contents. Defaults 3.2. striped_rows Whether draw alternating row stripes behind table. Defaults stripe setting used ggforestplot(). stripe_fill Fill colour used striped rows. Defaults stripe fill used ggforestplot(). stripe_colour Outline colour striped rows. Defaults stripe outline used ggforestplot(). grid_lines Whether draw black horizontal grid lines table. grid_line_colour Colour used table grid lines. grid_line_size Line width used table grid lines. grid_line_linetype Line type used table grid lines.

title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
text: patchwork-composed plot containing forest plot side table, ggplot add-object plot = NULL.

title: Add a Side Table to a Forest Plot — add_forest_table
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.3, -0.2, 0.4),   conf.low = c(0.1, -0.4, 0.2),   conf.high = c(0.5, 0.0, 0.6),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98),   p_value = c(0.012, 0.031, 0.004) )  p <- ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size", p.value = "p_value") add_forest_table(p, position = "left", show_n = TRUE, show_p = TRUE, estimate_label = "Beta")   ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size", p.value = "p_value") +   add_forest_table(position = "right", show_n = TRUE, show_p = TRUE, estimate_label = "Beta")

what: Add Split Tables Around a Forest Plot — add_split_table
title: Add Split Tables Around a Forest Plot — add_split_table
text: Compose split table blocks around forest plot text columns appear sides plotting panel. helper generally added last returns patchwork composition. Split tables draw table grid lines, left-side columns left-justified, right-side columns right-justified, default widths estimated displayed text reduce clipping.

title: Add Split Tables Around a Forest Plot — add_split_table
code: add_split_table(   plot = NULL,   show_terms = TRUE,   show_n = NULL,   show_estimate = TRUE,   show_p = FALSE,   left_columns = NULL,   right_columns = NULL,   term_header = "Term",   n_header = "N",   estimate_label = "Estimate",   p_header = "P-value",   digits = NULL,   text_size = NULL,   striped_rows = NULL,   stripe_fill = NULL,   stripe_colour = NULL,   left_width = NULL,   plot_width = NULL,   right_width = NULL )

title: Add Split Tables Around a Forest Plot — add_split_table
text: plot plot created ggforestplot(). Leave NULL use + add_split_table(...) syntax. show_terms Whether include term column default left-side selection left_columns supplied. show_n Whether include N column default left-side selection left_columns supplied. Defaults TRUE underlying plot data include n column. show_estimate Whether include formatted estimate confidence interval column default right-side selection right_columns supplied. show_p Whether include p-value column default right-side selection right_columns supplied. left_columns Optional explicit columns place left side forest plot. Accepts names "term" "n", positions 1:4 corresponding term, n, estimate, p. right_columns Optional explicit columns place right side forest plot. Accepts names "estimate" "p", positions 1:4 corresponding term, n, estimate, p. term_header Header text term column. n_header Header text N column. estimate_label Header label estimate column. p_header Header text p-value column. digits Number digits used formatting estimates p-values. Defaults 2. text_size Text size table contents. Defaults 3.2. striped_rows Whether draw alternating row stripes behind split table layout. Defaults stripe setting used ggforestplot(). stripe_fill Fill colour used striped rows. Defaults stripe fill used ggforestplot(). stripe_colour Outline colour striped rows. Defaults stripe outline used ggforestplot(). left_width Optional width allocated left table block. default estimated displayed left-side text long labels get room. plot_width Optional width allocated forest plot panel. default derived left right table widths. right_width Optional width allocated right table block. default estimated displayed right-side text.

title: Add Split Tables Around a Forest Plot — add_split_table
text: patchwork-composed plot containing left table, forest plot, right table, ggplot add-object plot = NULL.

title: Add Split Tables Around a Forest Plot — add_split_table
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.3, -0.2, 0.4),   conf.low = c(0.1, -0.4, 0.2),   conf.high = c(0.5, 0.0, 0.6),   sample_size = c(120, 115, 98),   p_value = c(0.012, 0.031, 0.004) )  p <- ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size", p.value = "p_value") add_split_table(   p,   left_columns = c("term", "n"),   right_columns = c("estimate", "p"),   estimate_label = "HR" )   ggforestplot(coefs, n = "sample_size", p.value = "p_value") +   add_split_table(left_columns = c(1, 2), right_columns = c(3, 4), estimate_label = "HR")

what: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: Standardizes coefficient table internal forest-plot data structure used throughout ggforestplotR.

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
code: as_forest_data(   data,   term,   estimate,   conf.low,   conf.high,   label = term,   group = NULL,   grouping = NULL,   separator_group = NULL,   n = NULL,   p.value = NULL,   exponentiate = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending") )

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: data data frame containing coefficient estimates intervals. term Column name holding model term identifier. estimate Column name holding point estimate. conf.low Column name holding lower confidence bound. conf.high Column name holding upper confidence bound. label Optional column name used displayed row label. group Optional column name used color-grouping multiple estimates per row. grouping Optional column name used split rows grouped plot sections. separator_group Optional column name used identify labeled variable blocks can outlined separator lines. n Optional column name holding sample sizes N labels table helpers. p.value Optional column name holding p-values. exponentiate Logical; TRUE, require positive values estimates intervals. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending".

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
text: standardized data frame ready ggforestplot() table composition helpers.

title: Standardize coefficient data for forest plots — as_forest_data
code: raw <- data.frame(   variable = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   beta = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   lower = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   upper = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  as_forest_data(   data = raw,   term = "variable",   estimate = "beta",   conf.low = "lower",   conf.high = "upper" ) #>        term estimate conf.low conf.high     label group grouping #> 1       Age     0.10     0.02      0.18       Age  <NA>     <NA> #> 2       BMI    -0.08    -0.16      0.00       BMI  <NA>     <NA> #> 3 Treatment     0.34     0.12      0.56 Treatment  <NA>     <NA> #>   separator_group    n p.value #> 1            <NA> <NA>      NA #> 2            <NA> <NA>      NA #> 3            <NA> <NA>      NA

what: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: Builds forest plot standardized coefficient data directly fitted model, optional grouped sections, striped rows, separator lines labeled variable blocks, controls point shape confidence-interval staples.

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
code: ggforestplot(   data,   term = "term",   estimate = "estimate",   conf.low = "conf.low",   conf.high = "conf.high",   label = term,   group = NULL,   grouping = NULL,   grouping_strip_position = c("left", "right"),   separator_group = NULL,   n = NULL,   p.value = NULL,   exponentiate = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending"),   point_size = 2.3,   point_shape = 19,   line_size = 0.5,   staple_width = 0.2,   dodge_width = 0.6,   separator_lines = FALSE,   separator_line_linetype = 2,   separator_line_colour = "black",   separator_line_size = 0.4,   striped_rows = FALSE,   stripe_fill = "grey95",   stripe_colour = NA,   zero_line = TRUE,   zero_line_linetype = 2,   zero_line_colour = "grey60" )

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: data Either tidy coefficient data frame model object supported broom::tidy(). term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, label, group, grouping, grouping_strip_position, separator_group, n, p.value Column names used data data frame. exponentiate Logical; TRUE, draw estimate axis log scale null line 1. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending". point_size Point size coefficient markers. point_shape Shape used coefficient markers. line_size Line width confidence intervals. staple_width Width terminal staples confidence interval lines. dodge_width Horizontal dodging used grouped estimates. separator_lines Logical; TRUE, draw dashed separator lines around labeled block identified separator_group. separator_line_linetype Line type used separator lines. separator_line_colour Colour used separator lines. separator_line_size Line width used separator lines. striped_rows Logical; TRUE, shade alternating rows. stripe_fill Fill color used shaded rows. stripe_colour Border color shaded rows. zero_line Logical; TRUE, draw null reference line. zero_line_linetype Line type null reference line. zero_line_colour Color null reference line.

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
text: ggplot object. Use standard ggplot2 functions labs() plot labels, add composition helpers styling main plot.

title: Draw a ggplot2 forest plot — ggforestplot
code: coefs <- data.frame(   term = c("Age", "BMI", "Treatment"),   estimate = c(0.10, -0.08, 0.34),   conf.low = c(0.02, -0.16, 0.12),   conf.high = c(0.18, 0.00, 0.56) )  ggforestplot(coefs)   ggforestplot(coefs, striped_rows = TRUE, point_shape = 17) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Basic forest plot")

what: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: Uses broom::tidy() convert fitted model forest-plot data.

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
code: tidy_forest_model(   model,   conf.int = TRUE,   conf.level = 0.95,   exponentiate = FALSE,   intercept = FALSE,   sort_terms = c("none", "descending", "ascending") )

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: model fitted model object supported broom::tidy(). conf.int Logical; TRUE, request confidence intervals broom::tidy(). conf.level Confidence level intervals. exponentiate Logical; passed broom::tidy(). intercept Logical; FALSE, drop intercept term. sort_terms sort rows: "none", "descending", "ascending".

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
text: standardized coefficient data frame ready ggforestplot().

title: Tidy a model object for forest plotting — tidy_forest_model
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars)  tidy_forest_model(fit) #>   term    estimate   conf.low   conf.high label group grouping separator_group #> 1   wt -4.35879720 -5.9006341 -2.81696034    wt  <NA>     <NA>            <NA> #> 2   hp -0.01782227 -0.0485098  0.01286526    hp  <NA>     <NA>            <NA> #> 3 qsec  0.51083369 -0.3888708  1.41053822  qsec  <NA>     <NA>            <NA> #>      n      p.value #> 1 <NA> 3.217222e-06 #> 2 <NA> 2.441762e-01 #> 3 <NA> 2.546284e-01  set.seed(123) logit_data <- data.frame(   age = rnorm(250, mean = 62, sd = 8),   bmi = rnorm(250, mean = 28, sd = 4),   treatment = factor(rbinom(250, 1, 0.45), labels = c("Control", "Treatment")) ) linpred <- -9 + 0.09 * logit_data$age + 0.11 * logit_data$bmi +   0.9 * (logit_data$treatment == "Treatment") logit_data$event <- rbinom(250, 1, plogis(linpred)) logit_fit <- glm(event ~ age + bmi + treatment, data = logit_data, family = binomial())  tidy_forest_model(logit_fit, exponentiate = TRUE) #>                 term estimate conf.low conf.high              label group #> 1                age 1.103890 1.061512  1.151411                age  <NA> #> 2                bmi 1.118858 1.043220  1.204389                bmi  <NA> #> 3 treatmentTreatment 3.133149 1.782915  5.613038 treatmentTreatment  <NA> #>   grouping separator_group    n      p.value #> 1     <NA>            <NA> <NA> 1.719452e-06 #> 2     <NA>            <NA> <NA> 2.093412e-03 #> 3     <NA>            <NA> <NA> 9.087778e-05

title: ggforestplotR 0.0.0.9000
text: Added grouped section support forest plots. Added alternating row striping. Added add_forest_table() side-table composition. Added point shape confidence-interval staple width controls. Added package documentation, tests, getting-started vignette.
